skill.name: qryma-search
skill.description: Search the web with multiple output formats using the Qryma API. Supports Markdown, JSON, and Brave search-like output formats.
skill.summary: Web search skill using Qryma API with flexible output formats for AI assistants.
skill.author: qryma-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 112 potential issues, but most are false positives. Environment variable access (QRYMA_API_KEY, QRYMA_ENDPOINT) is legitimate configuration for an API-based skill. Network calls target the documented Qryma API endpoint. External command findings are documentation examples in markdown, not executable code. Filesystem access is limited to standard config file locations (~/.qryma/.env). No malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Configuration
low_findings.description: Skill reads QRYMA_API_KEY and QRYMA_ENDPOINT from environment variables and ~/.qryma/.env config file. This is expected behavior for an API-based skill but requires user to trust the Qryma service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of standard API key loading pattern from environment variables and config file - legitimate for API client but requires trust in external service.

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External API
low_findings.description: Skill makes HTTPS POST requests to https://search.qryma.com/api/web endpoint, sending user search queries. This is core functionality but requires trust in the Qryma service with user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear evidence of network requests to documented Qryma API endpoint - expected behavior for search skill but data leaves user's environment.

content.user_title: Search the web with Qryma API
content.value_statement: Quickly find information online using the Qryma search engine. Get results in multiple formats including Markdown for readability, JSON for structured data, or Brave search-like format.
actual_capabilities: Execute web searches using the Qryma search API
Return results in Markdown format for readable output
Return results in JSON format for structured data processing
Return results in Brave search-like format with title, URL, and snippet
Support multiple languages via language code parameter
Enable safe search mode for filtered results
limitations: Requires a valid Qryma API key to function
Depends on external Qryma API availability and rate limits
Search results limited to what Qryma API returns
Cannot access content behind paywalls or login requirements

use_cases.title: Research Assistant
use_cases.description: Find information on topics quickly during coding or writing tasks. Search for documentation, tutorials, or latest information on technologies.
use_cases.target_user: Developers and writers who need fast access to current information

use_cases.title: Fact Verification
use_cases.description: Verify claims, check facts, or find authoritative sources on specific topics. Useful for ensuring accuracy in generated content.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and researchers who need to validate information

use_cases.title: Competitive Analysis
use_cases.description: Search for information about competitors, market trends, or industry developments. Export results in JSON for further analysis.
use_cases.target_user: Business analysts and product managers tracking market intelligence

prompt_templates.title: Basic Web Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for "how to learn Python programming" and return results in Markdown format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Simple search query with default settings for quick information lookup.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Result Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for "latest AI trends 2024" with max 10 results in JSON format for analysis.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Retrieving multiple results in structured format for further processing.

prompt_templates.title: Language-Specific Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for "machine learning tutorials" in Spanish (lang=es) with detailed results enabled.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Searching in specific languages with enhanced detail for non-English queries.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Search with Pagination
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for "quantum computing basics" with start offset 10, max 5 results, safe search enabled, in Brave format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Using pagination, safe search, and specific output format for curated results.

output_examples.input: Search for "Python best practices" with Markdown format
output_examples.output:
1. Python Best Practices Guide
   https://example.com/python-guide
   - Comprehensive guide covering PEP 8, code organization, and common patterns.

2. Writing Pythonic Code
   https://example.com/pythonic
   - Learn to write clean, idiomatic Python code with practical examples.

output_examples.input: Search for "climate change data" with JSON format
output_examples.output: Query returned structured JSON with title, URL, and content snippet for each result, suitable for programmatic processing or data analysis workflows.

best_practices: Always include specific, focused search terms for better results
Use Markdown format for human-readable output, JSON for programmatic use
Set appropriate max_results to avoid excessive API calls
anti_patterns: Do not use for searching sensitive or private information
Avoid excessive polling or automated high-frequency searches
Do not rely on search results for critical decisions without verification

faq.question: How do I get a Qryma API key?
faq.answer: Visit the Qryma website and sign up for an account. API keys are available from your account dashboard. The free tier provides generous limits for most use cases.

faq.question: Where is my API key stored?
faq.answer: Set the QRYMA_API_KEY environment variable, or create a .env file in the skill directory or ~/.qryma/.env with your key.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Three formats: Markdown (md) for readable text, JSON (raw) for structured data, and Brave-like format with title, URL, and snippet fields.

faq.question: Can I search in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --lang parameter with ISO language codes. Common options include es (Spanish), fr (French), de (German), and many others.

faq.question: What is the difference between safe search on and off?
faq.answer: Safe search filters out adult content and potentially offensive material. Enable with --safe flag for family-friendly or professional environments.

faq.question: How do I get more detailed results?
faq.answer: Use the --detail flag to enable detailed results. This may return additional metadata about each search result depending on API support.
